Petition To Reconsider The Order To Respondents Request For Surcharge; Attorneys Fees And Objection To Ordinary And Extraordinary Compensation Of Administrator And Attorney And For Sua Sponte Relief As To The Tax Surcharge
Off Calendar Aug-17-2018 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: James Bridgman for and with Brenda Lopez.
Dannielle Campbell for and with Remedios Esteves and Emilion Perez (Respondents/Beneficiaries).
Laura Villanueva (Court Interpreter) also present.
Victor Perez also present.

The Court inquired as to the certification status of the interpreter. The interpreter stated for the record: Her name is Laura Villanueva. Her Certification Number is 301670. She has an oath on file with the Court. The language to be interpreted is Spanish to English and English to Spanish.

Mr. Bridgman stated that Probate Examiner's notes from July 6, 2018 have been addressed by way of a supplement (copy provided), that the issue of the bank account for the insurance proceeds held jointly in the Attorney's names was thoroughly before the Court several times and has already been addressed, and that he is not sure why the Examiner is raising this issue. Mr. Bridgman also stated that the Internal Revenue Service waived the penalties and interest ($7,539.69) and it was reimbursed back to the Estate account, that the receipts for the File & Serve costs are attached to the supplement as exhibit E, requested that the CPA be paid in full, that the creditor's claims of Bank of America and American Express be paid, requested that the Estate be distributed 50% to his Client and 50% to the Respondents, presented oral argument to support his position regarding the Court granting his request for extraordinary fees and the Respondents paying for said fees, spoke of the surcharge hearing being brought in bad faith, requested that the Court sign the order for withdrawal of funds from the blocked account, etc. Ms. Campbell stated that she has no objection to payment of the Creditor's Claims or to the proposed distribution. Ms. Campbell also stated that she objects to fees for response to the Examiner's notes that ultimately were not fully responded to, that she objects to the extraordinary fees and the request to have her Clients pay said fees, and presented oral argument to support her position with respect to the surcharge hearing not being brought in bad faith. Mr. Bridgman and Ms. Campbell have no objection to the Court's suggestion of having an "omnibus clause" added to the order with respect to any undiscovered assets. Pursuant to the stipulation of the parties, the Court will remove the surcharge and penalties against Brenda Lopez and notes that this leaves interest in the amount of $410.00. The Petition to Reconsider the Order to Respondent's Request for Surcharge, etc. is under submission.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: R. Rosado. Not reported.

REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (842 32nd Street, Oakland, Ca 94608-4364)
Off Calendar Sep-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgman for (administrator) Brenda Lopez.

The Court stated that there are procedural issues. Mr. Bridgman stated that publication was filed. The Court stated that it indicates publication was on 8/31/16 but the petition states that the property was sold in a public sale on 8/22/16, referring counsel to section 4c. Mr. Bridgman stated that the buyer is here today, that everyone has been notified, that he has had difficulty in understanding what the Court wants, that the last time he was told to do it this way, that he made an error in not checking the box for commission, that a new notice was filed in August and provided a copy to the Court. The Court stated that the notice it has does not have a hearing date included and that the hearing date on this copy is written in ink and confirmed with counsel that the copy sent out had this hearing date on it. Mr. Bridgman stated that they are requesting a 5% commission, that the brokers have worked hard, that the buyer is Lamia Merced Fityan who received notice, that in regards to the blocked account there is a previous court order regarding a blocked account which has been set up and the funds will go into that account. The Court stated that it would like to see it, that there is an issue that funds can be released with the agreement of the defendants, that nothing on this says blocked account, that it will need to be set up and it nees to be on the order and indicating that it can only be released upon Court Order, and that the petition did not include the manner of vesting title. Mr. Bridgman replied that it is fee simple on the one individual. The sale for 100% interest was confirmed at $600,000.00 based on the terms and conditions as set forth in the report of sale filed 8/24/16. The Court granted the Petition to Confirm Sale of Real Property (subject to review of the order). Proceeds are required to be placed into a blocked account, and counsel is to make sure that the procedural issues are corrected. Mr. Bridgman stated that the contingency regarding the loan is removed.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputy Grimaldi. (Not Reported)

Petition To Vacate The Order For Sale Of The Property And To Sell And Confirm The Sale Of The Property To A New Buyer Pursuant To Ccp 10351
Off Calendar Jun-27-2016 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: James Bridgeman for petitioner Brenda Lopez; Clinton Killian for and with John Strong; Danielle Vogl for the beneficiaries.

The Court asked counsel to comment on whether any additional briefing or submissions would be needed. Mr. Bridgman replied that he did not believe any additional briefing was neeeded. He requested that the Court proceed to order the reversal of the sale of the property and have it again put up for sale to determine if there are any damages kto the estate. Mr. Killian stated that he had no objections to the court reversing the sale and allowing the property to be put back on the market. He stated that if the future sale is for a lower amount, it would not be due to anything done by his client as buyer, but because of affirmative misrepresentations and/or the failure of the seller/administrator to make disclosure of known facts which affect the sale value of the property. He stated that there was a failure to disclose the City of Oakland's owner occupancy requirement for the property, as well as the misidentification of the property as a triplex when the City of Oakland requires it to be used as a duplex. He added that if damages are sought against his client, he would demand an evidentiary hearing on the damages claim. Ms. Vogl stated that she also agreed that the sale should be vacated, but that she believes the estate should not be made to suffer any damages from the conduct of the administrator. The Court stated that he will grant the petition for reversal of the sale, subject to review of proposed orders submitted by Mr. Bridgman and Mr. Killian. A further status hearing re sale of the property was set for October 31, 2016 at 9:00 am in Dept. 204.

Judge: Andrew Y.S. Cheng. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Barrientos. (Not reported)

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Yulissa Zalaica for and with Remedios & Emilio Estevez. James Bridgman for and with Brenda Lopez.

The Court stated that there are competing petitions, one from a lawful spouse, one from the mother who has objected to Ms. Lopez's petitions, that the petition requested but did not name a professional administrator and that it would require an amended petition and notice. Mr. Bridgman reported that there has not been discussions between counsel, that they are concerned about the assets, that Victor (son of decedent) is collecting rent but not paying rent, that $60,000.00 is missing and that his client has priority. Ms. Zulaica reported that she spoke with Mr. Bridgman regarding a 3rd party administrator, that she has received their objections, that her client's position is that they do not feel Ms. Lopez is competent to serve, that they have witnesses regarding her incompetency and that there are conflicts and restraining orders. The Court asked if everyone was prepared to present argument on Ms. Lopez's petition as there is no proper competing petition. Ms. Zulaica stated that she would like to request an evidentiary hearing. Mr. Bridgman expressed his objections to an evidentiary hearing. Ms. Zulaica stated that she has requested discovery as of yesterday, that this is not about legal competence, that 2 of the 3 beneficiaries object to the appointment of Ms. Lopez and that to avoid costs they suggest a 3rd party administrator. The Court stated that it cannot ignore the request for an evidentiary hearing, but not without prejudice to a petition for special administration or the parties agreeing to a 3rd party administrator, that this is a complicated estate, that there are conflicts in the family and that if the objector wants to pursue a separate petition it would have to be a new petition with notice and provisions for bond. The Court set an evidentiary hearing for July 10, 2015 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204, and the 2 petitions for letters of administration were continued to the same date. Joint or separate statements regarding the issues to be decided must be filed by May 28, 2015. Designation of witnesses and documents must be filed by July 2, 2015. Prior to the July 10, 2015 hearing the parties are expected to exchange information and meet & confer regarding reaching a resolution.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Leslie Gomez. Bailiff: Deputies Lopes & Santiago. (Not Reported)
